· l)tate of Califor1l.ia 
Fair Political Practices Commissio1l. 
P.o. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Robert Kaplan 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Citizens for Property Rights 
987 University Avenue, *8 
Los Gatos, CA 95030 
Dear Mr. Kaplan: 
Administration 
322·5660 
executive/legal 
322·5901 
April 24, 1984 
Re: 
FPPC No. A-84-102 
Enforcement 
322~1 
This is in reply to your letter dated April 20, 1984, 
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
Citizens for Property Rights is a committee which was formed 
for the purpose of qualifying an initiative for the Los Gatos 
ballot.' 
You stated that the committee's "post-qualification 
report" (the first report required to be filed by a committee 
primarily formed for the qualification of a measure pursuant to 
Gov. Code Section 84200 (d» 
covered the qualification period of 
December 22, 1983 through March 20, 1984.* 
You asked whether 
cumulative amounts must be carried over from the first reporting 
period (the qualification period) to the second reporting 
period. 
For example, if a contributor gave $90 during the 
qualificat,ion period which ended March 20, 1984, and gave 
another $90 on March 25, would the next report contain a 
cumulative amount for that contributor of $90 or $180? 
Gov. Code Section 82018 states that if: 
••• pursuant to subdivision (d) of Section 84200, a filer 
is reqired to file a campaign statement in connection with 
the qualification of a measure and such qualification effort 
extends into two calendar years, the period over which the 
cumulative amount is calculated shall end on the closing 
date for the qualification statement • 
••• The next period over which the cumulative amount is 
calculated shall begin on the day after the closing date of 
such statement. 
Therefore, under Section 82018, the cumulative period for 
*The period covered by the qualification statement should be 
January 1, 1983 through March 20, 1984; it should not begin with 
December 22, 1983. 
See Gov. Code Section 82046(b) • 


Robert Kaplan 
April 25, 1984 
Page 2 
the qualification statement ends on March 20, and a new 
cumulative period begins on March 21. Using your example, if a 
person contributed $90 during the qualification period which 
ended March 20, and another $90 on March 25, the cumulative 
amount for the next report due after the qualification statement 
would be $9 O. 
Please call me if you have any questions. 
Sincerely, 
LJ. 
~~

--,fI~~ 
Jeanne Pr i tchard 
Political Reform Consultant

Sacramento, CA

Dear Jeanne, 
.. 
,-. 
I 
" . ' 
T c::ephone: 395·1)550 
fA 
This letter is in response to our telephone conversation of l8-Aoril-84. 
Our situation is this: 
Our 35 Day Post-Qualification Report covered the period 
December 22, 1983 through March 20, 1984. 
This report also contained information 
from the first reporting period of 1984 which went from January 1, 1984 through 
March 17, 1984. 
My specific question is whether cumulative totals from our first reporting period 
are to be carried over to the second reporting period? 
In other words, if 
Joe Shmo gave $90 total between December 22, 1983 and March 20, 1984 and another 
$90 on March 25, 1984, would the next report contain a cumulative total of 
$90 or $180? 
I look forward to a speedy reply. 
Si nc'ere 1 y, 
'~----
I .,' -.- -
" 
Rober~ ,'Kapl an 
Campaign Coordinator 
RK/sr

sections.question: You asked whether cumulative amounts must be carried over from the first reporting period (the qualification period) to the second reporting period. For example, if a contributor gave $90 during the qualification period which ended March 20, 1984, and gave another $90 on March 25, would the next report contain a cumulative amount for that contributor of $90 or $180?
sections.conclusion: Therefore, under Section 82018, the cumulative period for the qualification statement ends on March 20, and a new cumulative period begins on March 21. Using your example, if a person contributed $90 during the qualification period which ended March 20, and another $90 on March 25, the cumulative amount for the next report due after the qualification statement would be $90.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether cumulative contribution amounts from a measure qualification reporting period must be carried over to the next reporting period, or whether the cumulative calculation resets after the qualification period closes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under California Government Code Section 82018, cumulative amounts do not carry over between reporting periods for measure qualification campaigns. The cumulative period for the qualification statement ends on March 20, 1984, and a new cumulative period begins on March 21, 1984, so the second $90 contribution would be reported with a cumulative amount of $90, not $180.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated April 20, 1984) and FPPC response letter (dated April 24, 1984). FPPC response clearly marked with conclusion explaining Section 82018. Minor OCR issues in footer sections did not affect extraction of core Q&A content. FPPC also includes a footnote correction about the correct starting date for the qualification period.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether cumulative amounts must be carried over from the first reporting period (the qualification period) to the second reporting period. For example, if a contributor gave $90 during the qualification period which ended March 20, 1984, and gave another $90 on March 25, would the next report contain a cumulative amount for that contributor of $90 or $180?

CONCLUSION: Therefore, under Section 82018, the cumulative period for the qualification statement ends on March 20, and a new cumulative period begins on March 21. Using your example, if a person contributed $90 during the qualification period which ended March 20, and another $90 on March 25, the cumulative amount for the next report due after the qualification statement would be $90.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Citizens for Property Rights regarding campaign disclosure requirements for a measure qualification committee. The FPPC clarifies that cumulative contribution amounts reset after the qualification reporting period ends, so contributions in the subsequent reporting period are calculated separately.
